description_long: This warehouse is a large, rectangular industrial building with a brick exterior and a series of three gabled roof sections covered in dark gray material. The facade features large, evenly spaced windows with metal frames, providing ample natural light to the interior. A prominent concrete or metal silo structure is attached to one corner, suggesting storage or processing capabilities. The brickwork is accented by visible pilasters and lintels, and the windows are set in regular patterns along the longer sides. The rear and side elevations are simpler, with fewer windows and a more utilitarian appearance. The overall structure is robust and functional, typical of early-to-mid 20th-century industrial architecture.
description: A large brick warehouse with three gabled roofs, metal-framed windows, and an attached silo structure. The building features a robust industrial design with regular window patterns and utilitarian details.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main entrance area, large windows, and the attached silo structure prominently at the corner.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the brick facade, gabled roof sections, and a series of tall windows along the length of the building.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals a simpler elevation with fewer windows and the silo structure visible at the far end.
description_view_3: This view displays the opposite long side with more windows and the silo structure at the back corner.
attribute_facade_design: The main facade is constructed of red brick with regularly spaced, large metal-framed windows and a prominent industrial entryway. Decorative pilasters and lintels accentuate the window arrangements, and a concrete or metal silo structure is attached at one corner.
attribute_roof_structure: The roof consists of three large, parallel gabled sections covered in dark gray material, with a small cylindrical silo structure attached at one end. No skylights or rooftop equipment are visible.
